# Homebrew cask — generated by GoReleaser

A Homebrew **cask** is generated at release time by GoReleaser (see the
`homebrew_casks` section of `multiai-go/.goreleaser.yaml` — the old `brews`
formula config is deprecated since GoReleaser 2.10) with the real SHA256 of
each released macOS archive. On every `v*` tag it lands in:

```
dist/homebrew/Casks/multiai.rb
```

The generated cask includes a post-install hook that removes the macOS
quarantine attribute (`xattr`), because the binary is not
Apple-signed/notarized (0 EUR signing plan: sha256 checksums + Cosign
keyless instead).

## Auto-push behavior

`skip_upload` uses a GoReleaser template that checks the
`TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable:

| `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` | `skip_upload` | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Not set / empty | `true` | Cask generated in `dist/` only, not pushed |
| Set to a non-empty PAT | `false` | Cask generated AND pushed to the tap repo |

This means you can run `goreleaser release --clean` locally or in CI
without any special configuration — the cask is always produced. It is
only pushed when the token is explicitly provided.

## Enabling auto-push

1. Create the tap repository:
   `github.com/lrochetta/homebrew-tap`
2. Generate a classic GitHub PAT with `repo` scope from an account that
   has push access to the tap repository.
3. Add the PAT as a repository secret named `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` in the
   main `multiai` repository settings.
4. In the CI workflow (`release.yml`), add the secret to the GoReleaser
   step's `env:` block:
   ```yaml
   env:
     TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   ```

On the next `v*` tag, GoReleaser will push the updated cask to
`lrochetta/homebrew-tap` automatically.

## Manual publication (no token)

If you have not configured `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN`, the cask is still
generated in `dist/homebrew/Casks/multiai.rb`. Copy this file to the
`Casks/` directory of your tap repository:

```sh
cp dist/homebrew/Casks/multiai.rb /path/to/homebrew-tap/Casks/
cd /path/to/homebrew-tap
git add Casks/multiai.rb
git commit -m "multiai v$(git describe --tags)"
git push
```

## User installation

Once the cask is published in the tap, users install with:

```sh
brew tap lrochetta/tap
brew install --cask multiai
```

Or directly (Homebrew auto-taps on first access):

```sh
brew install --cask lrochetta/tap/multiai
```

Upgrade:

```sh
brew upgrade --cask multiai
```
